answer:
The debugger needs to write a breakpoint instruction (like int3) at the address corresponding to 0x77E81234 in the target process. Since the debugger mapped the same text segment starting at 0x01000000 in its own address space, the offset from the segment base is 0x1234, so the debugger’s virtual address is 0x01001234. To modify code, the page must be writable, so the debugger maps it with COPY-ON-WRITE access to avoid altering the original shared code.

\boxed{F}
